content.sanskrit: शीक्षां व्याख्यास्यामः । वर्णः स्वरः । मात्रा बलम् । साम सन्तानः । इत्युक्तः शीक्षाध्यायः ॥
content.transliteration: śīkṣāṁ vyākhyāsyāmaḥ | varṇaḥ svaraḥ | mātrā balam | sāma santānaḥ | ityuktaḥ śīkṣādhyāyaḥ ||
content.text: We shall expound Siksha (phonetics): phoneme, pitch, quantity, force, rhythm, and manner of junction — thus is the chapter on Siksha stated.
The knower of Brahman attains the supreme. This is the verse about it.

content.sanskrit: अन्नं ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात् अन्नाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते ।
content.transliteration: annaṁ brahmeti vyajānāt annāddhyeva khalvimāni bhūtāni jāyante |
content.text: He realised that food is Brahman. From food, verily, are all these beings born.

content.sanskrit: असन्नेव स भवति असद्ब्रह्मेति वेद चेत् अस्ति ब्रह्मेति चेद्वेद सन्तमेनं ततो विदुः ॥
content.transliteration: asanneva sa bhavati asadbrahmeti veda cet asti brahmeti cedveda santamenaṁ tato viduḥ ||
content.text: If one knows Brahman as non-existent, one becomes non-existent. If one knows Brahman as existing, then they know that he truly exists.

content.sanskrit: यतो वाचो निवर्तन्ते अप्राप्य मनसा सह । आनन्दं ब्रह्मणो विद्वान् न बिभेति कुतश्चन ॥
content.transliteration: yato vāco nivartante aprāpya manasā saha | ānandaṁ brahmaṇo vidvān na bibheti kutaścana ||
content.text: From where speech, together with the mind, turns back, unable to reach — he who knows the bliss of Brahman fears nothing at all.

content.sanskrit: अन्नं न निन्द्यात् तद्व्रतम् । प्राणो वा अन्नम् । शरीरमन्नादम् ।
content.transliteration: annaṁ na nindyāt tadvratam | prāṇo vā annam | śarīramannādam |
content.text: Do not despise food — that is the vow. The vital breath is food. The body eats food.
